questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Range of Motion' and its importance in sports. How does it affect athletic performance?
questions.answer: Range of Motion refers to the ability to move a joint through its full range of motion. It is essential in sports as it allows for efficient movement, reduces the risk of injury, and enhances athletic performance. A limited range of motion can lead to decreased performance, increased risk of injury, and decreased overall fitness.
marking_scheme: Definition of Range of Motion (1 mark)

questions.q: A person is performing a squat with a 20 kg barbell. If the person's power output is 200 W, what is the velocity of the barbell?
questions.answer:
Power = Work / Time = Force x Velocity
Given: Power = 200 W, Force = 20 kg x 9.81 m/s^2 = 196.2 N
We need to find velocity (v)
200 = 196.2 x v
v = 200 / 196.2 = 1.019 m/s

questions.q: Describe the concept of 'Proprioception' and its role in sports. How does it affect movement and athletic performance?
questions.answer: Proprioception refers to the ability to sense the position and movement of one's body. It plays a crucial role in sports as it helps athletes to maintain balance, coordinate movements, and make quick decisions. Poor proprioception can lead to decreased movement efficiency, increased risk of injury, and decreased overall athletic performance.
marking_scheme: Effect on movement and athletic performance (2 marks)

questions.q: A person is performing a deadlift with a 30 kg barbell. If the person's force is 300 N, what is the velocity of the barbell?
questions.answer:
Power = Work / Time = Force x Velocity
Given: Power = 300 N x v (we need to find v), Force = 30 kg x 9.81 m/s^2 = 294.3 N
We need to find velocity (v)
300 = 294.3 x v
v = 300 / 294.3 = 1.019 m/s

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Injury Prevention' in sports. What are some common methods used to prevent injuries?
questions.answer: Injury prevention in sports refers to the strategies and techniques used to reduce the risk of injury. Common methods include warm-up and cool-down exercises, stretching and flexibility exercises, strengthening exercises, and use of protective gear.

questions.q: A person is performing a bench press with a 25 kg barbell. If the person's power output is 220 W, what is the velocity of the barbell?
questions.answer:
Power = Work / Time = Force x Velocity
Given: Power = 220 W, Force = 25 kg x 9.81 m/s^2 = 244.75 N
We need to find velocity (v)
220 = 244.75 x v
v = 220 / 244.75 = 0.898 m/s
